old_value: {"id":"rc-0as","title":"Fix false positive secret detection: Generic API Key matches variable names like anthropic_api_key","description":"The Generic API Key pattern in both secret-patterns.ts and secret_patterns.py matches variable names containing api_key (e.g. anthropic_api_key, settings.anthropic_api_key) regardless of whether the value is actually a secret. The regex fires on any 16+ char string assigned to any variable containing api_key. Same issue affects Generic Secret pattern with password/secret/pwd. Fix: Variable-name matching is wrong signal. Detection should focus on VALUE characteristics. Known prefixes are handled by specific patterns. {"id":"rc-rf9","title":"GitHub Issues integration: scan results and natural text to issues","description":"Two-part GitHub Issues integration:

Part A - Rafter scan results to GitHub Issues: After scan completes, create GitHub issues for findings. Map each finding (secret, policy violation, audit flag) to structured issue with title, description, severity label, CWE/OWASP labels, file+line, remediation. Batch creation. Dedup by file+pattern hash. CLI: rafter issues create --from-scan <id>.

Part B - Natural text to GitHub Issues skill: Takes natural language input, converts to well-structured GitHub issue. Input: clipboard, pipe, file, inline text. Handles: Claude Code conversation, /security-review output, raw feedback. Uses LLM via MCP/skill context to extract title, description, labels, severity, affected files. CLI: rafter issues create --from-text or pipe. Skill: /rafter-issue in Claude Code/OpenClaw.

Infra needed: GitHub API client (gh CLI wrapper or octokit), issue template system, dedup logic. Both node/ and python/ implementations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"romethorstenson@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T22:35:10Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:35:10Z"}

{"notes":"AUDIT FINDINGS:

CODEX INTEGRATION ISSUES:
1. No Codex detection in agent init (Node or Python) - README/CLI_SPEC say it should detect ~/.codex
2. No --skip-codex flag in init command (CLI_SPEC documents it, not implemented)
3. No Codex skill installation code - README says skills go to ~/.agents/skills/rafter/ and rafter-agent-security/
4. No codex environment in config schema (only openclaw and claudeCode)
5. No Codex status in agent status command
6. TOML export format generates custom format, needs usage instructions for Codex sandbox config

OPENCLAW INTEGRATION:
1. Skill version 0.5.7 but CLI version is 0.5.8 (needs bump)
2. Backend skill version 0.5.6 (needs bump)
3. Otherwise solid - skill manager, 4 commands, 12-dimension audit all present

PLAN:
- Add Codex environment to config schema and defaults
- Add Codex detection + skill installation to Node init.ts
- Add Codex detection + skill installation to Python agent.py
- Add --skip-codex flag to both
- Add Codex status to Node status.ts
- Update Codex TOML export with sandbox policy format
- Bump skill versions to 0.5.8
- Update Python status to include Codex"}

old_value:
{"id":"rc-uos","title":"Tune API key variable name detection to avoid false positives on env var references","description":"The secret scanner (node/src/scanners/secret-patterns.ts and python/rafter_cli/scanners/secret_patterns.py) has a known false positive problem: it flags API_KEY variable NAMES (e.g. anthropic_api_key, settings.anthropic_api_key, GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY) even when the code is just referencing the env var name, not an actual secret value.

This is a real workflow problem — coding agents legitimately write code like:
- const apiKey = process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- os.environ['GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY']
- config.get('api_key')
- API_KEY_VAR = 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'

The Generic API Key pattern (lines ~99-110 in both implementations) matches any 16+ char string assigned to a variable containing 'api_key'. The Generic Secret pattern does the same for 'password/secret/pwd'.

Fix: Detection should focus on VALUE characteristics, not variable names. Options:
1) Require high-entropy values (Shannon entropy threshold) to distinguish real secrets from env var name strings
2) Whitelist common env var reference patterns (process.env.*, os.environ[], os.getenv(), config.get())
3) Only flag when the value looks like an actual key (hex, base64, prefixed tokens like sk-, ghp_, etc.)
4) Reduce severity of generic patterns to 'info' by default, let users promote via .rafter.yml

Previous polecat (rc-0as/obsidian) identified this exact issue. Tests should include false positive cases like anthropic_api_key and settings.password. See also: the existing specific patterns (AWS, GitHub, Stripe, etc.) which already handle real prefixed tokens correctly — the generic patterns are the problem.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"romethorstenson@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T22:36:24Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-04T22:36:24Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"attached_molecule: rc-wisp-z238
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-04T23:11:24Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The secret scanner (node/src/scanners/secret-patterns.ts and python/rafter_cli/scanners/secret_patterns.py) has a known false positive problem: it flags API_KEY variable NAMES (e.g. anthropic_api_key, settings.anthropic_api_key, GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY) even when the code is just referencing the env var name, not an actual secret value.

This is a real workflow problem — coding agents legitimately write code like:
- const apiKey = process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- os.environ['GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY']
- config.get('api_key')
- API_KEY_VAR = 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'

The Generic API Key pattern (lines ~99-110 in both implementations) matches any 16+ char string assigned to a variable containing 'api_key'. The Generic Secret pattern does the same for 'password/secret/pwd'.

Fix: Detection should focus on VALUE characteristics, not variable names. Options:
1) Require high-entropy values (Shannon entropy threshold) to distinguish real secrets from env var name strings
2) Whitelist common env var reference patterns (process.env.*, os.environ[], os.getenv(), config.get())
3) Only flag when the value looks like an actual key (hex, base64, prefixed tokens like sk-, ghp_, etc.)
4) Reduce severity of generic patterns to 'info' by default, let users promote via .rafter.yml

Previous polecat (rc-0as/obsidian) identified this exact issue. Tests should include false positive cases like anthropic_api_key and settings.password. See also: the existing specific patterns (AWS, GitHub, Stripe, etc.) which already handle real prefixed tokens correctly — the generic patterns are the problem."}

new_value:
{"design":"## Research Summary: claude-code-security-review

**Repo**: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-security-review (MIT, 3.5k stars)

**What it is**: GitHub Action using Claude to perform semantic security review of PR diffs. Python-based, uses Claude Code CLI subprocess for deep repo analysis.

**Key architecture**: PR diff + metadata → structured security prompt → Claude Code CLI → two-stage false positive filtering (hard regex rules + Claude API second-pass) → PR comments.

**Security categories scanned**: Input validation (SQLi, command injection, XXE, path traversal), Auth/Authz bypass, Crypto/secrets, Injection/RCE (deserialization, pickle, eval, XSS), Data exposure, Business logic (race conditions, TOCTOU), Config security, Supply chain.

**False positive filtering**: 17 exclusion precedents including: env vars are trusted, UUIDs unguessable, React auto-escapes XSS, client-side auth not needed, shell script injection rarely exploitable, SSRF only if controls host/protocol, AI prompt injection not a vuln, test files excluded.

**Rafter-cli insights**: (1) Embed filtering precedents in agent system prompts as proactive secure coding guidance, (2) Two-stage validation pattern (cheap regex + expensive LLM) for any agent output, (3) Confidence-gated output reduces noise, (4) Language/framework-aware exclusions, (5) Additive customization model for security policy.

**Research doc**: docs/research/claude-code-security-review.md"}

old_value: {"id":"rc-1qt","title":"Review rafter-cli agent init and propose secure-coding guardrails","description":"Review the existing rafter-cli codebase — specifically how 'rafter agent init' works, what gets injected into agent system prompts (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.), and the current security posture.
